summary: This is an exceptionally well-crafted test suite. Coverage is complete: every stated requirement (latest for main/master, pr-{number}, v{semver}, {branch}-{short-sha}, sanitization, error handling) has a dedicated Describe block with multiple It clauses. Rigor is equally strong — edge cases are exhaustive: case-insensitive main detection, PR numbers at boundaries (1, 99999), semver pre-release suffixes, multiple simultaneous tags, non-semver tags ignored, nested branch slashes, SHA truncation to exactly 7 chars, 128-char tag truncation, leading/trailing hyphens, consecutive hyphen collapse, empty-string-after-sanitization, invalid and too-short SHAs, and empty Tags arrays. Design is clean: TDD cycles are explicitly documented with RED/GREEN/REFACTOR comments, Describe blocks are logically grouped and named, tests are self-contained and independent, BeforeAll properly dot-sources the module, assertion style is idiomatic Pester, and integration tests at the end compose multi-requirement scenarios with count assertions. The only marginal gaps are the absence of shared fixtures (minor given test simplicity) and no explicit test for a negative PrNumber, but these do not materially affect confidence. This suite would reliably catch regressions across all specified behaviors.
